toolUseResult.originalFile:
require "test_helper"

class SandboxesControllerDiscoveryTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    @user = users(:one)
    @sandbox = sandboxes(:alice_running)
    sign_in_as(@user)
    DockerMock.reset!
    DockerMock.containers[@sandbox.container_id] = {
      "Id" => @sandbox.container_id,
      "State" => { "Status" => "running", "Running" => true }
    }
  end

  test "GET discover_files returns the rendered table" do
    DockerMock.exec_response = [ [ ".claude/.credentials.json\n.npmrc\n" ], [], 0 ]
    get discover_files_sandbox_path(@sandbox)
    assert_response :success
    assert_select "td", text: /\.claude\/\.credentials\.json/
    assert_select "td", text: /\.npmrc/
  end

  test "POST promote_file with action_type=bind creates a PersistedPath for the parent dir" do
    post promote_file_sandbox_path(@sandbox), params: {
      path: ".claude/.credentials.json", action_type: "bind"
    }
    assert_redirected_to sandbox_path(@sandbox, anchor: "discover")
    assert_not_nil @user.persisted_paths.find_by(path: ".claude")
  end

  test "POST promote_file with action_type=bind on top-level file uses path as bind dir" do
    post promote_file_sandbox_path(@sandbox), params: {
      path: ".somedir", action_type: "bind"
    }
    assert_not_nil @user.persisted_paths.find_by(path: ".somedir")
  end

  test "POST promote_file with action_type=inject reads content via exec and stores it" do
    DockerMock.exec_response = ->(_cmd) { [ [ "FILE BODY" ], [], 0 ] }
    post promote_file_sandbox_path(@sandbox), params: {
      path: ".npmrc", action_type: "inject"
    }
    inj = @user.injected_files.find_by!(path: ".npmrc")
    assert_equal "FILE BODY", inj.content
  end

  test "POST promote_file with action_type=ignore creates an IgnoredPath" do
    post promote_file_sandbox_path(@sandbox), params: {
      path: ".weird/file", action_type: "ignore"
    }
    assert_not_nil @user.ignored_paths.find_by(path: ".weird/file")
  end

  test "POST promote_file with unknown action returns alert" do
    post promote_file_sandbox_path(@sandbox), params: {
      path: ".x", action_type: "haxx"
    }
    assert_match(/Unknown action/, flash[:alert].to_s)
  end

  test "POST promote_file blocks foreign sandbox" do
    other = sandboxes(:bob_running)
    assert_raises(ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound, Pundit::NotAuthorizedError) do
      post promote_file_sandbox_path(other), params: { path: ".x", action_type: "ignore" }
    end
  end
end

content.text:
Done. **191 tests pass, 0 failures.** New code is rubocop-clean (the 2 lint hits are in pre-existing `set_smb_password`, not mine).

## What got built

| Layer | Files |
|---|---|
| Migrations | `db/migrate/20260416100001_create_injected_files.rb`, `..._create_persisted_paths.rb` (with .claude/.codex backfill for existing users), `..._create_ignored_paths.rb` |
| Models | `app/models/injected_file.rb` (encrypts content, validates path), `persisted_path.rb` (normalizes, validates), `ignored_path.rb`. `user.rb` gets the 3 associations + `after_create_commit :seed_default_persisted_paths` |
| Service | `app/services/sandbox_manager.rb` extended: public `inject_files` (post-start `docker exec`, mirrors `set_smb_password`), public `write_home_baseline`, dynamic `volume_binds`/`ensure_mount_dirs` for persisted paths. New `app/services/home_file_discovery.rb` with prefix/exact ignore lists + suggestion rules |
| Controllers | `settings_controller.rb`: `update_persisted_paths`, `update_injected_files`, `delete_injected_file`. `sandboxes_controller.rb`: `discover_files` (GET) + `promote_file` (POST) |
| Routes & policy | `routes.rb` 5 new routes; `sandbox_policy.rb` adds `discover_files?`/`promote_file?` |
| UI | `settings/show.html.erb` gains "Persisted Directories" + "Injected Files" sections (mode is octal — "600" → 0o600). `sandboxes/show.html.erb` gets a "Discover Files" link. New `sandboxes/discover_files.html.erb` table with Bind/Inject/Ignore action buttons. Stimulus: `persisted_paths_controller.js`, `injected_files_controller.js` |
| Image | `images/sandbox/entrypoint.sh` creates `/var/sandcastle/` so the baseline file has a home |
| Tests (51 new) | 19 model + 16 service + 16 controller across `test/models/{injected_file,persisted_path,ignored_path,user_persistence_seeding}_test.rb`, `test/services/{home_file_discovery,sandbox_manager_inject_files}_test.rb`, `test/controllers/{settings,sandboxes_controller_discovery}_test.rb`. DockerMock extended with `Container#exec` capture + configurable response |

## Live integration check

```
User: thies → persisted_paths: [".claude", ".codex"]   (seeded by migration)
Volume binds for thies's sandbox:
  /tmp/.../users/thies/persisted/.claude → /home/thies/.claude
  /tmp/.../users/thies/persisted/.codex  → /home/thies/.codex
```

Migration auto-backfilled `.claude` and `.codex` for the existing `thies` user; new user signups get them via `after_create_commit`.

## What "e2e" did and didn't cover

**Covered** (rails test, 191 runs):
- Full request → controller → service → DB cycle for every new endpoint
- DockerMock simulates `container.exec` so `inject_files`, `write_home_baseline`, and discovery's `find | comm` all execute the real production code paths and have their args asserted
- Encryption-at-rest verified by reading raw column bytes after writing plaintext

**Not covered** (would need a real Docker host):
- A real `docker exec` actually creating the file inside a sandbox
- The `comm -23` baseline diff against a real `find /home/$USER` output
- Browser-driven click-through of the Settings UI / Discover button

The dev compose stack (`sandcastle-pr81-postgres-1` etc.) is up but on the `pr81` branch's web image — running these flows live would mean rebuilding the web container off this branch (`mise run deploy:dev`) and exercising it from a browser. Happy to do that if you want — just say the word and I'll bring it up and walk through the discovery flow with you.

## One pre-existing test issue worth flagging

`test/services/sandbox_manager_test.rb` (the *original* tests, not mine) actually fail with `Errno::EROFS: /data` unless you set `SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR`. I worked around this with `SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR=/tmp/sandcastle-test-data` for the run. Worth either adding a default in `test_helper.rb` or making the directory creation idempotent in `prepare_traefik_config`. Not in scope for this task; flagging it because it'll bite future test runs.

diff --git a/images/sandbox/Dockerfile b/images/sandbox/Dockerfile
index e19b076..dcf513f 100644
--- a/images/sandbox/Dockerfile
+++ b/images/sandbox/Dockerfile
@@ -8,9 +8,25 @@ ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev
 
 # Always pull latest security patches and package updates.
 # CACHE_DATE is set at build time to bust the layer cache on demand.
+#
+# Hold Docker-stack packages before upgrading: the base image pins
+# containerd.io=1.7.27-1 (see Dockerfile.base for rationale — containerd.io ≥ 2.x
+# breaks sysbox-runc, nestybox/sysbox#958). Without the hold, `apt-get upgrade`
+# installs containerd.io 2.x, which also replaces /usr/bin/runc with a newer
+# runc (1.3.3+) whose CVE-2025-52881 fix rejects sysbox's FUSE-backed procfs
+# with "unsafe procfs detected" and breaks docker-in-docker inside sandboxes.
+# Re-install the pinned runc afterwards as belt-and-suspenders in case an
+# older base image (built before the apt-mark hold was added) is used.
 ARG CACHE_DATE=0
-RUN for i in 1 2 3; do apt-get update && break || sleep 5; done \
-    && apt-get upgrade -y && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
+RUN apt-mark hold docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin \
+    && for i in 1 2 3; do apt-get update && break || sleep 5; done \
+    && apt-get upgrade -y \
+    && RUNC_VERSION="v1.1.15" \
+    && ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture) \
+    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/download/${RUNC_VERSION}/runc.${ARCH}" \
+       -o /usr/bin/runc \
+    && chmod +x /usr/bin/runc \
+    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
 
 # Bake version into the image
 RUN echo "${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}" > /etc/sandcastle-version
diff --git a/images/sandbox/Dockerfile.base b/images/sandbox/Dockerfile.base
index 72947f1..cda8d65 100644
--- a/images/sandbox/Dockerfile.base
+++ b/images/sandbox/Dockerfile.base
@@ -84,6 +84,7 @@ RUN install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings \
          containerd.io=1.7.27-1 \
          docker-buildx-plugin=0.24.0-1~ubuntu.24.04~noble \
          docker-compose-plugin=2.40.3-1~ubuntu.24.04~noble \
+    && apt-mark hold docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin \
     && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
 
 # Pin runc — multiple runc versions break inside sysbox containers:

content.text:
Hold up — I noticed two unexpected modifications that aren't mine and need your call before I commit:

- **`images/sandbox/Dockerfile`** and **`images/sandbox/Dockerfile.base`** — they have substantive changes (`apt-mark hold` for the docker stack + a runc reinstall, with a comment about CVE-2025-52881 / nestybox/sysbox#958). I didn't touch these — they were uncommitted in your tree before this session started even though `git status` showed clean at conversation start. Looks like prior work from another session.
- **`db/schema.rb`** and **`db/queue_schema.rb`** — these did get touched by `bin/rails db:migrate`. The diff includes my new tables *plus* other rows (`image_built_at`, `smb_enabled`, `custom_links` …) that were missing from the schema files because they hadn't been regenerated after earlier migrations. Bringing them into sync is necessary for my migrations to land cleanly.

How do you want to handle the Dockerfile changes?

1. **Bundle them into my commit** — they're related to sandbox image hardening, but they're really a separate fix (sysbox/runc compat) and the commit message would have to cover two unrelated topics.
2. **Commit them separately first** — I draft a `fix:` commit just for the Dockerfile changes (with the runc-pin/sysbox rationale already in their inline comment), then my `feat:` commit for everything else.
3. **Leave them uncommitted** — only commit my work; you handle the Dockerfile changes later.

I'd lean toward **option 2**: clean separation, easier to revert either independently. Want me to do that?
